# UC-23.5.4 · Delivery SLA Compliance and Last-Mile Performance

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Performance, Business &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We line up what people were told about when a box would arrive with what record shows happened at the door—street by street and lane by lane—without mixing that picture with dock receipts or invoice bundles studied elsewhere.*

---

## Description

Splunk-ingested parcel timelines marry storefront-promised clocks from commerce-side extracts with carrier-direct scan ladders—FedEx Track REST payloads exposing trackResults[0].scanEvents[*].eventType alongside UPS activity[].status.code chains and DHL shipment.events[*].statusCode timelines—then normalize SCAC aliases with eval-driven carrier buckets before grading whether doorstep arrival beats checkout-facing commitments after grace windows tuned per Next-Day versus Ground lanes. TMS overlays sourced from project44 Movement shipment feeds or FourKites parity APIs stitch multimodal hops where Heavy Forwarder polls pull REST bodies every five minutes while webhook-first lanes push Quantum View Notify XML or DHL-signed JSON straight into HTTP Event Collector receivers guarded by HMAC headers so Splunk correlates OD-to-DL dwell separately from fulfillment lag tracked elsewhere.

## Value

Operations leaders defending ShipStation-style ninety-seven-percent industry-on-time benchmarks against MetaPack ninety-six-percent US pacing—while budgeting exception remediation tied to Convey/project44 eight-percent delivery-exception averages—use carrier-by-carrier scorecards before CFO-approved refunds balloon; CX leadership protects promoter curves when zip-level clusters spike, and logistics procurement renegotiates lane rates when LaserShip handoffs versus FedEx Ground diverge materially on first-attempt success rather than debating anecdotal screenshots from broker inboxes.

## Implementation

(1) Stand HEC receivers with scripted HMAC verification for FedEx Track Notification SOAP XML, UPS Quantum View Notify, and DHL webhook JSON using x-dhl-signature; (2) deploy DB Connect pulls for OMS shipment promises plus modular inputs polling FedEx OAuth-secured `/track/v1/trackingnumbers`, UPS `/api/track/v1/details/{trackingNumber}`, DHL MyDHL tracking GETs, and TMS adapters hitting project44 `/api/v3/tracking/shipments/{id}` on five-minute cadence; (3) publish CSV lookups `carrier_service_levels.csv`, `carrier_exception_codes.csv`, `holiday_calendar.csv`, `customer_tier.csv`, `refund_policies.csv`; (4) schedule the bundled multi-stage SPL hourly with PagerDuty bridges for sustained sub-ninety-five-percent on-time slices and Slack #logistics-ops digests plus ServiceNow automation for zip-cluster exception bursts; (5) rehearse validation against FedEx Tracking Manager, UPS Quantum View, DHL MyDHL shipment pages, project44 Movement dashboards, and native OMS shipment journals.

## Evidence

Saved search `lastmile_carrier_scorecard_hourly` plus Dashboard Studio workbook `BA-LASTMILE-SLA` archived weekly beside hashed webhook receipts proving HMAC verification stayed enabled.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads aligned with splunkVersions above; Heavy Forwarder pairs provisioned with outbound HTTPS toward FedEx OAuth (`apis.fedex.com/oauth/token`), UPS OAuth (`https://api.ups.com/security/v1/oauth/token`), DHL tenant endpoints documented under MyDHL API portals, USPS Web Tools HTTPS gateways after USPS administrator approvals, plus TMS SaaS URLs issued by project44, FourKites, Shippeo, or MacroPoint administrators.
- Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens isolated per ingress lane—FedEx Track Notification SOAP payloads bound to `/services/collector/event` listeners verifying x-fedex-signature when operations publish signing secrets through KMS-backed macros; UPS Quantum View Notify XML routed through parallel stanza verifying x-ups-token hashes whenever brokerage contracts mandate issuer secrets; DHL webhook JSON guarded via x-dhl-signature HMAC-SHA256 comparisons documented inside DHL Express webhook onboarding packs.
- Splunk DB Connect identities scoped to SELECT projections exposing Shopify Fulfillment shipment promises, Salesforce Commerce Cloud order extracts (`dw.order.OrderMgr.searchOrders()` equivalents surfaced through JDBC-friendly replicas), Manhattan Active Omni REST snapshots mirrored nightly, SAP Commerce `/odata` clones, IBM Sterling fulfilment extracts—never embedding SYSDBA passwords inside searchable bundles.
- Lookup governance calendars refreshed weekly—carrier_service_levels.csv pairs carrier_norm plus svc_raw literals into buckets (`next_day`, `two_day`, `standard`, `economy`, `same_day`) aligning FedEx First Overnight versus UPS Next Day Air Early versus DHL Express Worldwide nuances against FedEx Ground or UPS Ground slower arcs; carrier_exception_codes.csv maps PU/OD/DL/DL-analog codes across carriers into normalized_exception taxonomy covering delivery_address_invalid, customer_not_home, refused, weather, damaged, signature_required; holiday_calendar.csv enumerates federal observances affecting business_days_taken math; customer_tier.csv ties shopper identifiers to cltv_band plus refund_policy_id; refund_policies.csv lists pct_refund_on_late plus free_shipping_credit_value for finance-approved escalation tiers.
- Roles granting `srchIndexesAllowed` across `business` without exposing unrelated payroll clones residing beside JDBC warehouses.

### Step 1 — Multi-carrier onboarding plus webhook subscriptions plus lookup upkeep
Stand up modular inputs executing OAuth client_credentials POST exchanges feeding FedEx Track REST POST `/track/v1/trackingnumbers` JSON envelopes enumerating trackResults[0].scanEvents[*].eventDescription, eventType, scanLocation postal intersections, exceptionCode pairs analogous to FedEx Web Services SOAP parity checks historically validated across Tracking API v17/v18 migrations—persist raw bodies under sourcetypes `fedex:tracking_rest`. Mirror UPS bearer workflows retrieving trackResponse.shipment[0].package[0].activity[*].status.description alongside `.status.code` letters `P`, `M`, `X`, `MV`, `D` documented inside UPS Tracking Integration guides—landing sourcetype `ups:tracking_json`. Subscribe Quantum View Notify feeds pushing asynchronous XML parcels Splunk forwards unchanged aside from newline trimming.

Schedule TMS collectors polling project44 Movement `/api/v3/tracking/shipments/{id}` surfaces emitting normalized keys (`eventTimestamp`, `eventStatusCode`, `stopType`, `exceptionType`) alongside FourKites lane equivalents every five minutes via Splunk Add-on patterns documented per carrier handshake PDFs—parameterize cron `*/5 * * * *` with jitter so overlapping shards avoid HTTP 429 storms.

Provision DB Connect pulls referencing Manhattan Active Omni REST mirrors (`GET /order/{orderId}/shipments`), Shopify webhook replicas (`fulfillment_events` tables), Salesforce Commerce Cloud JDBC exports, Kibo shipment fact tables—stamp `_time` from operational eventTimestamp or OMS promised clock, not indexer arrival.

Document webhook replay testing: regenerate DHL webhook signing keys quarterly, rehearse UPS token renewals hourly, archive sample FedEx SOAP Track Notification envelopes for AppSec review proving XML parser hardening stays current.

Maintain lookup hygiene: nightly diff `carrier_exception_codes.csv` when carriers publish refreshed code tables; monthly review `carrier_service_levels.csv` when peak surcharges redefine service marketing names; weekly import USPS/FedEx suspension bulletins into supplemental lookup feeding macro exclusions during hurricanes resembling Helene-scale outages or Texas freeze repeats.

Props.conf sketches on indexers parsing webhook JSON bodies:

```
[fedex:track_hec]
KV_MODE = json
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIME_PREFIX = "(eventTimestamp|scanTimestamp|actualDeliveryTimestamp)\\":\\"
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 40
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q%z
TRUNCATE = 1048576
```

Add `FIELDALIAS-track = tracking_number AS trackingNumber` plus parallel aliases mapping `carrier`, `service_level`, `promised_delivery_at_iso`, `actual_delivery_at_iso`, `exception_code` across sourcetypes; `TRANSFORMS-uni_lastmile` can stamp `dest_zip` from JSON `scanLocation.postalCode` or OMS mirrors before searches run.

### Step 2 — SPL pipelines plus KV safeguards
Paste the bundled SPL atop `index=business` unions covering carrier REST sourcetypes (`fedex:tracking_rest`, `ups:tracking_json`, `dhl:express_track`), TMS feeds (`tms:p44_shipment`, `tms:fourkites_shipment`), OMS extracts (`oms:shopify_ship`, `oms:sfcc_ship`, `oms:manhattan_ship`). Initial eval stanza coalesces heterogeneous identifiers (`tracking_number`, `order_id`, `carrier_norm`, `svc_bucket`)—critical before dedup because Shopify Shipping emits uppercase GUIDs while SAP emits VBELN strings.

Carrier normalization collapses synonyms (`fedex`, `fx`, `fed ex`) via case(match(...)) ladders—extend with lowercase feeds from LaserShip rebranded OnTrac blends flagged `regional_parcel`. Service buckets consume lookup overlays whenever merchandising strings drift (“UPS Next Day Air Early” vs marketing shorthand).

Business-day subtraction subtracts weekend_days heuristic placeholders—swap with precise macros referencing holiday_calendar.csv keyed by promised_delivery_at epoch mapped through strftime `%Y-%m-%d`. sla_met comparisons incorporate grace_days fractions reflecting noon-promises versus end-of-day anchors negotiated per tier.

streamstats counts repeated customer_not_home normalized_exception readings across sliding windows sized ten scans—approximating carrier attempt counters absent native attempt integers.

eventstats aggregates on_time_pct slices grouped carrier_norm × svc_bucket × hourly buckets feeding threshold filters isolating sustained breaches shorter dashboards ignore.

KV collections optional when CSV lookups exceed fifty thousand rows—migrate carrier_exception_codes into KV Store collections replicated cluster-wide with nightly backups scripted outside Splunk where governance mandates immutable copies.

### Step 3 — Validation cross-checks against portals
FedEx Tracking Manager UI: compare Splunk-derived OD/DL pairing timestamps against FedEx consumer-facing timelines for thirty-sample tracking_numbers weekly—ensure timezone offsets declared inside Splunk macros (`America/Memphis` vs shopper-local) reconcile portal screenshots Ops attaches.

UPS Quantum View dashboards: reconcile Quantum View Notify arrival sequences versus SPL aggregates—especially signature-required reroutes flagged MV scans mistaken as misses.

DHL Express MyDHL shipment tabs: validate PU→OK transitions mirror Splunk-normalized milestones—cross-check NH codes versus misroutes (`MS`).

project44 Movement dashboards: verify TMS-fed exceptionType spikes coincide with Splunk `tms:p44_shipment` anomaly rows—correlate carrier_SCAC swaps when shipments reroute through tertiary regional hauliers.

OMS reconciliation: Manhattan Active Omni shipment journals versus Splunk promised_delivery_at anchors—ensure merchant countdown edits surfaced overnight propagate JDBC extracts within SLA stated inside DB Connect polling cadence contracts.

Document variance thresholds—Splunk `_time` minus portal `_event_time` beyond fifteen minutes triggers InfoSec ticket referencing Splunk `_indextime` drift diagnostics.

### Step 4 — Alert routing plus crisis choreography
PagerDuty Events API v2 integration triggers only when sustained four-hour windows keep carrier_norm × svc_bucket on_time_pct below ninety-five percent—implement rolling summary-index snapshots verifying duration before paging VP Logistics.
Slack channel `#logistics-ops` receives hourly digest attachments summarizing worst dest_zip cohorts pulled from SPL filters referencing zip-prefix truncation macros.
ServiceNow scripted alerts open INC tickets automatically when exception clusters exceed seven parcels sharing normalized_exception=`weather` inside identical dest_zip prefixes inside rolling six-hour spans—populate short descriptions citing Splunk correlation IDs for audit trails.
Append CFO-approved refund narratives referencing refund_policies.csv outputs whenever revenue_pool breaches nightly budgets.

### Step 5 — Known pitfalls distinct from benign telemetry drift
Carrier scan sequencing inversion—Delivered publications arriving ahead of Out-for-Delivery logs because handheld units buffered barcode taps offline throughout suburban loops—scorecards relying on lexicographic ordering miscompute SLA attainment unless SPL sorts strictly by scan_ts ascending.
Neighbor-locker Delivered proofs satisfy carrier timestamps hours before occupants retrieve parcels—Splunk punctuality reads compliant yet shopper frustration persists until CX surveys corroborate delayed retrieval unrelated to parcel-network tardiness.
Commerce countdown mismatches FedEx Ground four-day matrices versus storefront teasers promising forty-eight-hour shopper optics—Splunk anchors promised_delivery_at from Shopify banner extracts rather than unpublished FedEx contractual matrices.
Address-exception rework loops flagged INVALID-ZIP corrections penalize carriers unfairly unless normalized_exception splits shopper-entered faults versus carrier transcription slips.
Friday-next-day pledges stretching across weekends confuse naive twenty-four-hour spans—FedEx Home Delivery Saturday eligibility contradicts UPS Ground weekend exclusions unless svc_bucket macros incorporate carrier-published calendars.
Force-majeure toggles freeze guarantees region-wide yet JDBC snapshots omit appended waiver codes until Ops paste supplemental macros referencing NOAA disaster polygons tied to hurricane seasons.
Early Out-for-Delivery scans preceding lengthy curb waits distort experiential KPIs compared with statutory SLA adherence measured midnight-to-midnight.
Undeliverable returns rerouted after refusal spikes normalized_exception counters though eventual doorstep successes qualify alternate fulfillment centers—first-attempt failures diverge materially from final disposition successes unless SPL tiers attempts separately.
Multi-carton shipments splitting deliveries across weekdays contradict package-level TMS milestones keyed child parcels—worst-leg aggregation macros must collapse onto parent order_id tuples fed by Manhattan Sterling merges.
FedEx SmartPost final-mile USPS transitions swap tracking_numbers mid-flight— joins ignoring USPS continuation identifiers orphan Delivered proofs altogether.
SLA-clock definitional drift—manifest timestamps preceding pickup scans by forty-eight hours exaggerate latency when planners mistakenly anchor clocks at label print versus warehouse pickup versus first-network injection.
Fulfillment-delay versus parcel-delay splits confuse analysts blending Manhattan pick-complete timestamps with UC scoped purely on parcel-network phases unlike OTC-cycle dashboards elsewhere.
Signature-required detention loops inflate exception counts despite shopper-approved alternate pickup appointments logged solely inside CSR desktops absent JDBC replication.
Photo-pod Delivered confirmations collide with shopper theft claims filed downstream—Splunk punctuality stays green though CX refunds erupt unless claims-feed joins augment SPL.
Carrier-hub timestamps stamped Memphis CDT opposing shopper-local PST pledges skew perceived breaches whenever macros omit timezone normalization bridging Rockies corridors versus Atlantic storefront messaging.
Dynamic reroutes toward LaserShip/Veho lanes triggered after surcharge thresholds breach procurement limits resemble degradation unless SPL annotates deliberate reroute reasoning sourced from TMS reroute_reason codes.

## SPL

```spl
index=business earliest=-30d@d latest=now
| eval tracking_number=tostring(trim(lower(coalesce(trackingNumber,TRACKING_NO,parcel_id,t_number,""))))
| eval dest_zip=tostring(trim(coalesce(dest_zip,DEST_ZIP,receiverPostalCode,toPostalCode,SHIP_TO_ZIP,"")))
| eval order_id=tostring(trim(coalesce(order_id,sfcc_order_id,shopify_order_gid,SALES_ORDER_NO,"")))
| eval carrier_raw=tostring(trim(lower(coalesce(carrier,carrier_scac,CARRIER_CODE,""))))
| eval carrier_norm=case(match(carrier_raw,"fedex|fx"),"fedex",match(carrier_raw,"ups|upsn"),"ups",match(carrier_raw,"dhl"),"dhl",match(carrier_raw,"usps"),"usps",match(carrier_raw,"ontrac|lasership"),"regional_parcel",match(carrier_raw,"shipbob|veho|axlehire|onerail|roadie|gls"),"regional_parcel",carrier_raw)
| eval svc_raw=tostring(trim(lower(coalesce(service_level,SERVICE_DESC,SERVICE_CODE,""))))
| eval svc_bucket=case(match(svc_raw,"overnight|next_day|priority overnight|first overnight|next day air early"),"next_day",match(svc_raw,"2day|second day"),"two_day",match(svc_raw,"same day|same-day"),"same_day",match(svc_raw,"economy|surepost|smartpost"),"economy","standard")
| lookup carrier_service_levels.csv svc_raw carrier_norm OUTPUT svc_bucket AS svc_bucket_csv
| eval svc_bucket=coalesce(svc_bucket_csv,svc_bucket)
| eval promised_delivery_at=coalesce(strptime(promised_delivery_at_iso,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q%z"),strptime(promised_delivery_clock,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"),strptime(PROMISED_DELIVERY_DT,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval calendar_date=strftime(promised_delivery_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup holiday_calendar.csv calendar_date OUTPUT fed_observed AS fed_holiday
| eval scan_ts=coalesce(strptime(scanTimestamp,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"),strptime(eventTimestamp,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"))
| eval actual_delivery_at=coalesce(strptime(actual_delivery_at_iso,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"),strptime(DELIVERED_TS,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval exception_code_raw=tostring(trim(lower(coalesce(exception_code,carrier_exception,EXCEPTION_REASON,""))))
| lookup carrier_exception_codes.csv exception_code_raw carrier_norm OUTPUT normalized_exception AS norm_exc
| eval norm_exc=coalesce(norm_exc,"unknown")
| eval cust=tostring(trim(coalesce(customer_id,shopper_ref,SOLD_TO_ID,"UNKNOWN")))
| lookup customer_tier.csv cust OUTPUT cltv_band refund_policy_id
| lookup refund_policies.csv refund_policy_id OUTPUT pct_refund_on_late free_shipping_credit_value
| eval pct_refund_on_late=tonumber(coalesce(pct_refund_on_late,"0"))
| eval order_value=tonumber(coalesce(order_value,ORDER_TOTAL,"0"))
| eval grace_days=case(svc_bucket="next_day",0.5,svc_bucket="same_day",0.15,svc_bucket="two_day",1.25,svc_bucket="standard",2.5,1)
| eval holiday_flag=if(match(dest_zip,"^430|^441|^972|^381"),1,0)
| eval weekend_days=if((strftime(promised_delivery_at,"%w") IN ("0","6") OR strftime(actual_delivery_at,"%w") IN ("0","6")),1,0)
| eval business_days_taken=if(isnotnull(actual_delivery_at) AND isnotnull(promised_delivery_at),round(((actual_delivery_at-promised_delivery_at)/86400)-weekend_days-if(isnotnull(fed_holiday),tonumber(fed_holiday),0),1),null())
| eval sla_met=case(isnull(actual_delivery_at),0,isnull(promised_delivery_at),0,norm_exc IN ("delivery_address_invalid","customer_fault_address"),1,norm_exc IN ("weather","force_majeure") AND holiday_flag=1,1,business_days_taken<=0,1,isnotnull(business_days_taken) AND business_days_taken<=grace_days,1,1=1,0)
| eval revenue_at_risk=case(business_days_taken>1 AND sla_met=0 AND isnotnull(order_value),order_value*pct_refund_on_late,business_days_taken<=1 OR sla_met=1,tonumber(coalesce(free_shipping_credit_value,"0")),1=1,0)
| eval window_bucket=strftime(coalesce(scan_ts,_time),"%Y-%m-%d %H:00")
| sort tracking_number scan_ts
| streamstats current=f window=10 global=f count(eval(norm_exc="customer_not_home")) as failed_attempts by tracking_number
| stats earliest(scan_ts) as first_scan latest(scan_ts) as last_scan dc(norm_exc) as exc_mix sum(eval(if(norm_exc="customer_not_home",1,0))) as nh_attempts values(order_id) as orders_seen max(business_days_taken) as max_bd latest(actual_delivery_at) as actual_delivery_at latest(promised_delivery_at) as promised_delivery_at latest(sla_met) as sla_met_ship avg(order_value) as avg_order_value sum(revenue_at_risk) as revenue_at_risk_sum latest(norm_exc) as last_exc latest(svc_bucket) as svc_bucket latest(carrier_norm) as carrier_norm latest(cust) as customer_id max(failed_attempts) as peak_failed_attempts latest(window_bucket) as window_bucket by tracking_number dest_zip svc_bucket
| eval od_dl_hours=round((actual_delivery_at-first_scan)/3600,2)
| stats sum(eval(if(sla_met_ship=1,1,0))) as on_time_hits count as parcels sum(revenue_at_risk_sum) as revenue_pool avg(avg_order_value) as avg_ord_val avg(max_bd) as avg_lateness_dc avg(peak_failed_attempts) as avg_failed_attempts dc(tracking_number) as parcels_dc avg(od_dl_hours) as avg_od_dl_hours by carrier_norm svc_bucket dest_zip window_bucket
| eval on_time_pct=if(parcels>0,round(100*on_time_hits/parcels,2),null())
| eval exception_rate=round(if(parcels>0,100*(parcels-on_time_hits)/parcels,0),2)
| eval failed_first_attempt_rate=round(avg_failed_attempts*12,2)
| where on_time_pct < 95 OR exception_rate > 5 OR failed_first_attempt_rate > 8 OR avg_lateness_dc > 1.25
| eval rank_score=sqrt(abs(coalesce(on_time_pct,50)-95))+exception_rate+failed_first_attempt_rate
| sort limit=500 - rank_score
| table carrier_norm svc_bucket dest_zip window_bucket parcels_dc on_time_pct exception_rate failed_first_attempt_rate avg_lateness_dc avg_od_dl_hours revenue_pool avg_ord_val rank_score
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio lattice pairing splunk.singlevalue tiles for fleet on-time percentage versus ShipStation ninety-seven-percent benchmark overlay; heatmap carrier_norm × svc_bucket colored by on_time_pct; splunk.table ranking zip-prefix clusters when rank_score climbs; timeline overlay plotting OD-to-DL spans versus promised_delivery_at anchors; choropleth faceting regions after joining truncated dest_zip prefixes.

## Known False Positives

Carrier handheld queues occasionally upload Delivered legs ahead of Out-for-Delivery milestones—Splunk timelines sorted solely by ingestion order inflate punctuality unless scans reorder by canonical scan_ts tied to Memphis hub clocks versus shopper curb expectations. Checkout banners promising truncated transit windows still cite fulfillment-delay buffers baked two seasons earlier—Splunk compares storefront countdown modules rather than refreshed parcel-commit matrices published internally by carriers. Weather-throttled hubs suspend published guarantees regionally yet JDBC-fed promised_delivery_at stamps ignore disaster carve-outs until planners paste advisory overrides—noise accumulates unless lookup rows ingest USPS/FedEx published suspension notices.
